name: Skilled Nursing & Long-Term Care Cleaning
criticalZone: resident rooms, common areas, dining, assisted bathing
centralRisk: outbreak — C. difficile, norovirus, influenza — in a vulnerable resident population
regulatoryFramework: 105 CMR 150 — Massachusetts DPH, Standards for Long Term Care Facilities
CMS Requirements of Participation for Long-Term Care Facilities (F-tags)
CDC guidance for C. difficile and norovirus in long-term care settings
EPA List N registered disinfectants, including List K for C. difficile spores
h1Pattern: Skilled Nursing & Long-Term Care Cleaning in {city}, MA

commonSurveyFindings.finding: No effective infection prevention and control program — surveyors cite what they observe directly: hand hygiene missed between residents, isolation precautions not implemented, shared resident-care equipment (glucometers, blood-pressure cuffs, lifts, shower chairs) not cleaned and disinfected between residents, soiled linen handled or stored improperly.
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: The most-cited F-tag nationally. Computed from the CMS Health Deficiencies file (June 2026 refresh): 7,377 of 13,841 facilities nationally (53.3%) and 152 of 296 Massachusetts facilities (51.4%) were cited on their most recent standard health survey.

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Food storage, preparation and service not sanitary — soiled can openers, ice machines and cooler gaskets, unclean prep surfaces, undated or uncovered food, dish-machine sanitiser at the wrong concentration.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: F812 — 42 CFR 483.60(i), Food Procurement, Store, Prepare, Serve Sanitary
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: Second most-cited nationally: 6,341 of 13,841 facilities (45.8%) and 98 of 296 in Massachusetts (33.1%). Usually cited at wide scope, meaning the surveyor found it affected most or many residents.

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment not honoured — soiled floors and furnishings, odours in resident rooms, stained or torn privacy curtains and mattresses.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: F584 — 42 CFR 483.10(i), Safe, Clean, Comfortable, Homelike Environment
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: 2,346 of 13,841 facilities nationally (16.9%) and 51 of 296 in Massachusetts (17.2%).

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Facility environment not maintained safe, functional and sanitary — soiled utility and housekeeping rooms, unsanitary tub and shower rooms, damaged or unsealed surfaces that can no longer be properly cleaned.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: F921 — 42 CFR 483.90(i), Safe, Functional, Sanitary, Comfortable Environment
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: 824 of 13,841 facilities nationally (6.0%); 3 of 296 in Massachusetts (1.0%).

commonSurveyFindings.finding: No effective pest control programme — evidence of flies, ants, roaches or rodents, or a programme that is not maintained or documented.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: F925 — 42 CFR 483.90(i)(4), Maintains Effective Pest Control Program
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: 427 of 13,841 facilities nationally (3.1%); 6 of 296 in Massachusetts (2.0%). Often cited at wide scope.

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Essential equipment not maintained in safe operating condition, including dietary, laundry and resident-care equipment found broken, soiled, or not maintained to the manufacturer's instructions.
commonSurveyFindings.citation: F908 — 42 CFR 483.90(d)(2), Essential Equipment in Safe Operating Condition
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: 554 of 13,841 facilities nationally (4.0%); 9 of 296 in Massachusetts (3.0%).

commonSurveyFindings.finding: Bloodborne pathogens programme deficiencies — exposure control plan, regulated waste and contaminated laundry handling, decontamination of work surfaces and equipment, sharps containers, PPE and training.
commonSurveyFindings.frequency: Federal OSHA, October 2024 to September 2025: 43 citations across 10 inspections of skilled nursing facilities — the single most-cited standard for the sector.

_publishGate: Requires >=10 faqs and >=6 commonSurveyFindings before this service may go live. Survey findings are real regulatory citations — never invented, never inferred from the regulation text. Source: data/_todo-clinical-input.md.
